pattern: Storing words as a tree of shared character edges makes prefix and exact-word queries linear in the query length, independent of how many words are stored.
hints: Words that share a beginning should share storage for that beginning.
Model each node as a map from a single character to a child node, and mark the nodes where a complete word ends.
insert walks/creates a path of child nodes and flags the final node as a word-end; search walks the path and checks that flag; startsWith walks the path and only checks that it exists.

edge_inputs.description: insert a word, then exact-search and prefix-search around it
a stored prefix is not a stored word until inserted
one word is a prefix of another; both must search true
queries on an empty trie are all false

stress.description: many inserts then a mix of search/startsWith over overlapping vocabulary
stress.note: small alphabet and shared vocab force prefix overlap and word/prefix distinctions
